---
title: "Spatterdock (Nuphar advena)"
slug: "nuphar-advena"
group: "Plantae"
image: "https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/631130593/medium.jpg"        # keep for thumbnails
image-alt: "Spatterdock"  # keep for a11y
tax_kingdom: "Plantae"
tax_class: "Magnoliopsida"
tax_order: "Nymphaeales"
tax_family: "Nymphaeaceae"
categories: ["Plantae", "Magnoliopsida", "Nymphaeales", "Nymphaeaceae"]   # these drive the chips + filtering
freeze: true
---

<figure class="species-figure">

<img src="https://inaturalist-open-data.s3.amazonaws.com/photos/631130593/medium.jpg" alt="Spatterdock" class="species-photo"/>

<figcaption class="photo-credit">

Photo: <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/people/coreytcallaghan">
coreytcallaghan</a> (CC BY-NC) via <a href="https://www.inaturalist.org/observations/346249535">iNaturalist</a>

</figcaption>

</figure>

## Taxonomy

-   **Class:** Magnoliopsida
-   **Order:** Nymphaeales
-   **Family:** Nymphaeaceae

## Occurrence at DeLuca Preserve

-   [DeLuca Bio Blitz Species Observations](https://www.inaturalist.org/observations?project_id=191727&subview=map&taxon_id=82504)

## Conservation & rarity context

-   IUCN: Not Listed
-   **Rarity note:** Common species in Florida found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats.

## Natural history notes

An aquatic plant characterized by its large, heart-shaped floating leaves and yellow, cup-shaped flowers held above the water surface. In Florida, it is found in still or slow-moving freshwater habitats such as ponds, lakes, and marshes. It grows during the warmer months and provides cover and habitat for aquatic wildlife.

## References / links

-   [iNaturalist species page](https://www.inaturalist.org/taxa/82504-Nuphar-advena)
-   [Local profile](https://www.flawildflowers.org/flower-friday-nuphar-advena/)
